sentence_reviews.text: Parameter-efficient fine-tuning adapts a frozen or mostly frozen model through small trainable modules; LoRA learns low-rank updates to selected weight matrices.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: LoRA arXiv abstract lines 42-44 says LoRA freezes pretrained model weights and injects trainable rank-decomposition matrices into Transformer layers, greatly reducing trainable parameters for downstream tasks.
sentence_reviews.notes: The LoRA-specific part is directly supported; small trainable modules is a fair generalization from injected trainable matrices.

sentence_reviews.text: The role of Parameter-efficient tuning, adapters & LoRA is to connect Supervised fine-tuning & instruction tuning, Linear algebra to downstream capabilities including Large language models, Code models, copilots & repository intelligence, Model merging & weight-space composition.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: LoRA arXiv abstract lines 42-44 supports adaptation of pretrained LLMs using rank-decomposition matrices and experiments on GPT-style models; code models and model merging are not directly covered in the inspected evidence.
sentence_reviews.notes: LLM adaptation and linear algebra are supported; code/repository and model-merging downstream claims need narrower sources.

sentence_reviews.evidence_location: LoRA arXiv title and abstract lines 42-44 address low-rank adaptation of large language models, including GPT-3 and other language models.

sentence_reviews.text: substrate for Code models, copilots & repository intelligence
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: LoRA arXiv abstract lines 42-44 supports LLM adaptation broadly, but does not mention code models, copilots, or repository intelligence.
sentence_reviews.notes: No inspected source supports this downstream example.

sentence_reviews.text: substrate for Model merging & weight-space composition
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: LoRA arXiv abstract lines 42-44 supports low-rank adaptation matrices but does not discuss model merging or weight-space composition.
sentence_reviews.notes: LoRA adapters can be composed in practice, but the inspected packet source does not directly support this downstream edge.

sentence_reviews.text: Results are sensitive to data quality, hyperparameters, compute budget, random variation, and evaluation contamination. Its direct dependencies include Supervised fine-tuning & instruction tuning, Linear algebra.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: LoRA arXiv abstract lines 42-44 supports rank-decomposition matrices, trainable parameters, and downstream-task adaptation; it does not directly state the generic sensitivity/contamination limitation.
sentence_reviews.notes: Dependencies are partly supported; generic limitations require stronger sourcing.

sentence_reviews.text: Parameter-efficient tuning, adapters & LoRA uses Supervised fine-tuning & instruction tuning as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: LoRA lines 42-44 describes adaptation/fine-tuning for downstream tasks; Ouyang lines 38-40 supports supervised instruction fine-tuning as an LLM adaptation stage.
sentence_reviews.notes: PEFT/LoRA can implement supervised fine-tuning, but LoRA is not limited to instruction tuning.

sentence_reviews.text: Parameter-efficient tuning, adapters & LoRA uses Linear algebra as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: LoRA arXiv abstract line 43 describes trainable rank-decomposition matrices, directly invoking matrix factorization/linear algebra.

sentence_reviews.text: Parameter-efficient tuning, adapters & LoRA uses Affine transforms & dense layers as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: LoRA arXiv abstract line 43 says trainable rank-decomposition matrices are injected into each layer of the Transformer architecture; the abstract does not explicitly name affine/dense layers.
sentence_reviews.notes: LoRA commonly modifies linear weight matrices; the inspected abstract supports layers and matrices, not the exact atlas label.

edge_reviews.notes: LoRA supports parameter-efficient downstream fine-tuning; using it specifically for instruction tuning is common but not inherent, so core strength is somewhat broad.
Rank-decomposition matrices directly support a core linear algebra dependency.
Transformer layer matrices make affine/dense layers a defensible common dependency, though the abstract does not name affine transforms.
